title: Keisha Castle-Hughes
content:
{{Top|rwp}} {{Person |image=[[File:KeishaCastleHughesIMDB.jpg]] |name=Keisha Castle-Hughes |birth=[[March 24]], [[1990]]
[[Wikipedia:Donnybrook, Western Australia|Donnybrook]], [[Wikipedia:Western Australia|Western Australia]], [[Wikipedia:Australia|Australia]]{{WebCite|url=https://www.nzherald.co.nz/nz/from-virgin-mary-to-mum/K62GZKWEKFOHQQVWTVNCR5O67M/?c_id=1&objectid=10404883|text=From Virgin Mary to mum|author=Cook, Stephen and Marshall, Jonathan|date=2006-10-08|archivedate=20231023180233}} |death= |listedpronouns= |nationality=[[Wikipedia:New Zealanders|New Zealander]]
([[Wikipedia:Māori people|Māori]]/[[Wikipedia:English Australians|Australian]]) |occupation=Actress |companies= |website= }} {{Quote|We came to Australia only the day before filming. We did the last costume fitting -- which was only the second costume fitting -- so it was very nerve-wracking for them just in case things didn't fit. But it all went fine. I liked the whole look of the thing. It made me feel really proud to be wearing it.|Keisha Castle-Hughes|{{SWArchive|url=episode-iii/bts/production/beacon161.html|text=Homing Beacon #161 – ''Revenge''{{'s}} Royalty}}}} '''Keisha Castle-Hughes''' (born [[March 24]], [[1990]]) is a [[Wikipedia:New Zealanders|New Zealand]] actress who played [[Queen]] [[Apailana]] in [[Star Wars: Episode III Revenge of the Sith|''Star Wars'': Episode III ''Revenge of the Sith'']], and voiced [[Emerie Karr]] in ''[[Star Wars: The Bad Batch]]''. ==Biography== ===Early life=== Keisha Castle-Hughes was born in [[Wikipedia:Donnybrook, Western Australia|Donnybrook]], [[Wikipedia:Western Australia|Western Australia]] to [[Wikipedia:Māori people|Māori]] mother, Desrae Hughes, of [[Wikipedia:Ngāti Porou|Ngāti Porou]], [[Wikipedia:Tainui|Tainui]], and [[Wikipedia:Ngāpuhi|Ngāpuhi]] descent, and [[Wikipedia:English Australians|Australian]] father, Tim Castle. Castle-Hughes and her family moved to [[Wikipedia:Auckland|Auckland]], [[Wikipedia:New Zealand|New Zealand]] when she was four. She became a New Zealand citizen in [[2001]]. Castle-Hughes attended Bailey Road School, and later [[Wikipedia:One Tree Hill College|Penrose High School]]. ===Career=== Castle-Hughes received her [[Wikipedia:Academy Awards|Academy Award]] nomination for Best Actress in [[2004]] for her role in the film [[Wikipedia:Whale Rider|''Whale Rider'']]. While she did not win the award, she was at the time the youngest person nominated for the category, as well as the second Polynesian to be nominated for an Oscar. However, she won the [[Wikipedia:Critics' Choice Movie Award|Broadcast Film Critics Association Award]] for Best Young Actor/Actress for the role. In the same year, Castle-Hughes appeared in [[Wikipedia:Prince (musician)|Prince's]] controversial music video "[[Wikipedia:Cinnamon Girl (Prince song)|Cinnamon Girl]]."{{WebCite|url=http://blogcritics.org/archives/2004/10/19/144715.php|text="Cinnamon Girl" Video Controversy Caps Prince's Outstanding Year|date=2004-10-19|archivedate=20090427145335}} In [[2006]], Castle-Hughes played with [[Oscar Isaac]] in [[Wikipedia:The Nativity Story|''The Nativity Story'']], in which they portrayed the roles of [[Wikipedia:Mary (mother of Jesus)|Mary]] and [[Wikipedia:Saint Joseph|Joseph]]. In [[2009]], Castle-Hughes appeared with [[Rena Owen]] ([[Taun We]] in [[Star Wars: Episode II Attack of the Clones|Episode II]] and [[Nee Alavar]] in Episode III) in the TV movie [[Wikipedia:Piece of My Heart (film)|''Piece of My Heart'']]. She played the young version of Owen's character. In [[2011]], Castle-Hughes also shared the screen with [[Rohan Nichol]] ([[Captain]] [[Raymus Antilles]] in Episode III) in the film [[Wikipedia:Red Dog (film)|''Red Dog'']], and with [[Rachael Leigh Cook]] (voice of [[Jaesa Willsaam]] in the ''[[Star Wars: The Old Republic]]'' [[Star Wars video games|video game]]) in the film [[Wikipedia:Vampire (2011 film)|''Vampire'']] the same year. From 2015 to 2017, she appeared in [[Wikipedia:Game of Thrones|''Game of Thrones'']] alongside [[Jessica Henwick]]. In 2016, she worked on [[Wikipedia:Roadies (TV series)|''Roadies'']], a television series produced by [[J.J. Abrams]], including on an episode directed by [[Jon Kasdan]]. ==Filmography== {|class="sortable" {{Prettytable}} |- bgcolor="#CCCCCC" ! Year !! Title !! Series !! (episode)|Metamorphosis]]"||''[[Star Wars: The Bad Batch]]''||[[Emerie Karr]]{{TBB|Metamorphosis (episode)}} |- |2023||"[[The Outpost]]"||''Star Wars: The Bad Batch''||Emerie Karr{{TBB|The Outpost}}
